<current_datetime>2026-05-16T03:16:03.676Z</current_datetime>

I have an xUnit v3 project with several test classes. I need to run only the Smoke tests from classes whose name contains 'Integration' — I want a single combined filter expression for dotnet test rather than separate flags.

input.initialPrompt: I have an xUnit v3 project with several test classes. I need to run only the Smoke tests from classes whose name contains 'Integration' — I want a single combined filter expression for dotnet test rather than separate flags.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to know how to construct a single combined filter expression for `dotnet test` that:
1. Runs only tests with a "Smoke" category/trait
2. From classes whose name contains "Integration"

They want this as a single `--filter` expression rather than separate flags.

Let me look at their test file to understand the structure better, then provide the appropriate filter expression.

data.content:
Use this single `--filter` expression:

```bash
dotnet test --filter "ClassName~Integration & Category=Smoke"
```

This combines two conditions with `&` (AND):
- **`ClassName~Integration`** — class name contains "Integration" (matches `PaymentGatewayIntegrationTests` and `OrderIntegrationTests`)
- **`Category=Smoke`** — the `Trait("Category", "Smoke")` attribute

This will run 3 tests: `ProcessPayment_ValidCard_Succeeds`, `RefundPayment_ValidTransaction_Succeeds`, and `PlaceOrder_ValidItems_CreatesOrder` — skipping the Regression tests and anything in `PaymentValidationTests`.
